home.tagline: Computer Science Student · Future Game Developer
home.bio: Student in 2nd year of Computer Science, specialized in video game development.
home.aboutText1: For a few years now, I have learned programming through the creation of many projects (some of them on the portfolio)
home.aboutText2: Today, I specialize in programming video games, participating in various personal and academic projects. So that in a near future, I can integrate an independent studio.

footer.description: French student passionate about video game development.

libet.title: LIBET
libet.description: 3D Game at the first person in which the player embodies 'Libet', an old man with Alzheimer, living his life while he gradually loses reason.

projectMycoria.title: Project Mycoria
projectMycoria.description: 3D game of exploration and narrative wandering in the Livradois-Forez region. Play as Vincent Morel, a DGSI officer, charged with committing to a series of mysterious disappearances.

iplat.title: IPlat - My First Video Game
iplat.description: Short 2D platform game developed in 1 week with the Löve2D framework.

context.title: Context
context.paragraph1: IPlat is the first game I created in 9th grade. It is a quite simple 2D platform.
context.paragraph2: The player aims to move forward on each screen until the end of the game.
context.paragraph3: Obtaining coins add one more health point.
context.paragraph4: When the player no longer has health points they are teleported to the beginning of the current screen.
context.problemText: How to create a short 2D platformer without knowledge in less than a week ?

objectives.title: Objectives
objectives.item1: Discover and master the basics of the Löve2D framework
objectives.item3: Understanding the fundamental mechanics of a platform game (physics, collisions, inputs)
objectives.item4: Deliver a playable and functional game within a short period
objectives.item5: Publish my first game on a distribution platform (Itch.io)

missions.title: Main Tasks
missions.item2: Development of the physics engine and collisions

academic.title: Academic Incomes
academic.intro: Although realized outside the academic framework, this project has enabled me to acquire essential skills that have been useful to me in my training in Computer Science:
academic.projectManagementText: Organization of work and respect of deadlines

conclusion.title: Conclusion and Learning
conclusion.paragraph1: IPlat was a success for what I was able to learn during the creation, but from a different point of view. the game is quite short, and uses that assets found on the Internet, so I could have done better.
conclusion.paragraph2: This project reinforced my passion for developing video games and gave me the desire to continue creating and learning.

botSteam.title: Discord Steam Bot
botSteam.description: Bot discord allowing to display Steam account information, and link its own account.

webSoutenances.title: Defense Management Web Application
webSoutenances.description: Web application developed to facilitate the management of defenses at the University.

therapieMiroir-AR.title: Augmented Reality - Mirror Therapy
therapieMiroir-AR.description: Augmented Reality application based on Mirror Therapy for the re-education of stroke victims.

projetVapeur.title: Steam Project
projetVapeur.description: Steam-inspired web application, reproducing its essential principles and functionality.

but.title: University Bachelor of Technology in Computer Science
but.institution: University Institute of Technology of Le Puy-en-Velay
but.description: C++ Programming, Databases, Project Management, Unreal Engine and Unity

bac.title: High School Diploma - Specialities Computer Science, English
bac.description: High School Diploma with specialities Computer Science and English.

experience.emptyText: I am currently looking for an internship or a first professional experience in the development of video games.

cpp.title: C++ / C# Programming
cpp.description: Main languages used daily. C++ for low level programming and game engines, and C# with Unity for gameplay development.

gameEngines.title: Game Engines
gameEngines.description: Practical experience on Unity and Unreal Engine (as well as other engines) through several university and personal projects. Create gameplay systems, 3D asset integration, blueprint and C# scripting.

versionControl.title: Version & Project Management
versionControl.description: Daily use of Git and GitHub for team-based collaboration. Organization of projects with Jira: assignment of tasks, sprints, and follow-up of progress within an agile framework.
